paper_title: Asymptotic structure. II. Path-width and additive quasi-isometry
summary: Conjecture 1.2 from arXiv:2509.09031 proposes that for any graph class closed under edge contraction and edge subdivision, every (L,C)-quasi-isometry to a member of the class can be upgraded to a (1,C')-quasi-isometry. The source paper itself proves the special case where the class consists of graphs of bounded path-width. No post-October-2025 paper specifically resolving the full conjecture was found in a 5-call web search. A March 2025 paper (arXiv:2503.00798) on K_{2,3}-induced minor-free graphs establishes a related quasi-isometry result for bounded tree-width classes, but it predates this conjecture's statement.
search_queries: quasi-isometry graph class edge contraction subdivision multiplicative additive distortion Nguyen Scott Seymour 2025
notes: The conjecture is ≤ 1 year old (October 2025). The paper proves the bounded path-width special case. arXiv:2503.07448 (March 2025) reportedly disproves a conjecture about multiplicative-to-additive distortion improvement for general graphs, but that is distinct from Conjecture 1.2 which restricts to classes closed under edge contraction and subdivision. arXiv:2503.00798 (Chakraborty, March 2025) on K_{2,3}-induced minor-free graphs predates 2509.09031 and so cannot be a follow-up. No verified post-statement resolution was found.
